summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the variation of N/Ti ratio across different conditions: 'With native oxide', 'Before mod.', 'After mod.', and 'IR Annealing'.

data_extraction.a:
|Process step| N/Ti Ratio | 
|---|---|
| With native oxide| 0.95 | 
| Before mod. |0.98 | 
| After mod. |0.75 | 
| IR Annealing | 1.00 |

a.question: Why is the N/Ti ratio lower with native oxide?
a.answer: a	Oxygen atoms are also bonded to Ti atoms, resulting in less N-Ti bonds so lower N/Ti ratio.

a.question: Why does the modification step reduce the N/Ti ratio?
a.answer: a	With the modification chlorine is added to the surface, reducing the number of N-Ti bonds at the surface for which the XPS is sensitive.

a.question: Does the stoichiometry of the bulk of the film change after the ALE provess?
a.answer: a	No the stoichiometry of the bulk does not change as the bulk ratio is 1 and after IR anenealing as wel.

a.question: If the etched sample is stored in ambient conditions, what would be teh N-Ti ratio after a while?
a.answer: a	In that case the surface will be oxidized again, meaning a native oxide layer will have formed so  a N/Ti ratio of 0.95 will be measured.
